summary: High‑quality audience recording captures an explosive second set highlighted by a smoking Shakedown→Playing→China Doll sequence and a soaring Morning Dew.
review: The tape delivers near‑soundboard clarity, with only occasional waviness and a slightly recessed keyboard mix in the second set. Musically the show is a fan favorite – the Shakedown “poke‑around” jam, the seamless segue into Playing in the Band, China Doll and The Wheel, plus an unforgettable Morning Dew, make this one of the era’s standout performances despite a few rough spots early on.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~3.9; most comments praise both the show and the recording, with many noting near‑soundboard quality and several standout jams, outweighing a few negative remarks.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear and crisp; many listeners think it could be soundboard material. A slight “wavy” texture appears at times and the keyboards sit low in the mix during the second set, but overall the tape is highly listenable.

standout_songs: Shakedown > Playing in the Band > China Doll
show_quality.setlist_flow: The second set erupts with a pre‑drums jam that flows into a legendary Shakedown‑Playing‑China Doll sequence and culminates in The Wheel and a monumental Dew.  The first set is a bit ragged, but the energy builds dramatically, delivering an unforgettable night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Usually lauded for soaring leads on Dew and Wheel; a few reviewers note his solo sounding faster/sloppier early in the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for stepping up when other parts slipped, delivering solid rhythm and vocal work throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards are low in the mix during the second set but later add atmospheric shading on China Doll and Playing in the Band.
band_member_comments.Drums: The pre‑drums segment of the second set is highlighted as a grin‑inducing highlight.
